courseTitle: Multiplayer, Networked Play, and Live Operations
title: Online Lobby and Networked Prototype
genericDescription: Implement basic client/server or peer synchronization; handle latency visibly; maintain authoritative state where appropriate; manage lobby flow.
specificDescription: Build a small networked game or technical prototype with at least two connected players or simulated clients. Include create/join lobby flow, ready state, connection status, disconnect handling, latency indication, and a test log.
gradingRubric: 30 pts networking implementation; 20 pts synchronization quality; 20 pts lobby/connection handling; 15 pts playability; 15 pts technical documentation.
